touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Finn Evans introducing our AI sales assistant platform and its forecasting capabilities. No response received.
Follow-up cold email to Finn Evans highlighting case study on improving forecast accuracy for healthcare IT companies. No response received.
LinkedIn message sent to Finn Evans mentioning mutual connection and forecasting pain points in mid-market sales orgs. No response received.

Qualification call with Finn Evans. He confirmed they're struggling with pipeline visibility and forecast accuracy across their 30-person sales team. Currently using spreadsheets and Fireflies for call recording. Interested in automation and AI-powered insights. Agreed to loop in COO Dakota for strategic alignment.

questions_asked.question: What's your biggest challenge with sales forecasting right now?
questions_asked.answer: We're flying blind honestly. Reps update Salesforce sporadically and our weekly forecast calls are just guesswork. Dakota's been pushing for better visibility.

questions_asked.question: Are you using any tools today for call intelligence or deal tracking?
questions_asked.answer: We have Fireflies recording calls but nobody has time to review them. It's just a transcript graveyard.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform actually improve forecast accuracy?
questions_asked.answer: We analyze every customer interaction—calls, emails, meetings—and surface risk signals and engagement patterns that predict deal outcomes. It's like having an analyst review every deal.

next_steps: Finn to review pricing page and feature overview
Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Finn Evans. Left voicemail mentioning upcoming discovery call. No response.

Follow-up email to Finn with discovery call agenda and ROI calculator showing potential $180K in prevented slippage. Finn responded confirming attendance and asking to include Levi Okafor from their team.

questions_asked.question: Can Levi Okafor join the discovery call? He manages our frontline team and will be key to implementation.
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, the more stakeholders the better. I'll send him a separate intro email.

next_steps: Add Levi to discovery call invite

touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Finn Evans to confirm discovery logistics. No answer.
Cold email to Finn with pre-discovery questionnaire about their sales process and tech stack. Finn responded with details on their 3-stage sales process and current Salesforce + Fireflies setup.
next_steps: Review questionnaire responses before discovery

touches.ai_summary: Brief qualification call with Finn to clarify budget authority and decision process. Confirmed Dakota Richter (COO) has final approval, procurement handled by Ravi Mensah in RevOps. Budget cycle ends in 45 days. Finn mentioned they're also talking to Fireflies about expanding their existing contract.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through how a purchase like this gets approved at BlueIntelligence.
questions_asked.answer: I'd champion it, Levi would validate it works for the team, and Dakota gives the final yes. Ravi in RevOps handles contracts and vendor stuff.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd like something in place by end of quarter. We have budget allocated but it expires in about 6 weeks.

objections: We already have Fireflies—trying to understand what you do that they don't.
next_steps: Send competitive comparison doc
Schedule discovery call with Levi included

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email after qualification call with Fireflies comparison doc and discovery call agenda. Finn confirmed he reviewed the materials and is excited to dig deeper on the forecasting accuracy features.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for tomorrow

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Finn. Walked through their current process: 30 reps, 3-stage sales cycle, averaging 45-day deals. Biggest pain is deal slippage—they miss their forecast by 20-30% each month. Finn explained Dakota is tired of surprises in board meetings. Discussed how our AI surfaces early warning signals.

questions_asked.question: What happens when a deal slips today? How do you find out?
questions_asked.answer: Usually the rep tells me on Thursday that the deal they forecasted for Friday is actually next month. By then it's too late to do anything.

questions_asked.question: Does this require our reps to do anything differently or is it automatic?
questions_asked.answer: It's automatic. We connect to your calendar, email, and call recording tools. Reps don't change their workflow at all.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: If we could get forecast accuracy above 80% and Dakota stops asking me why deals are slipping, I'd be thrilled.

next_steps: Send technical integration requirements to Levi
Schedule technical evaluation with Levi's team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call with Finn to review discovery notes and confirm next steps. Finn mentioned Dakota is skeptical about another tool after Fireflies underdelivered, but willing to see a demo. Agreed to position this as a forecasting solution, not just call intelligence.

questions_asked.question: What's Dakota's biggest concern about adding new tools?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, adoption. We bought Fireflies and nobody uses it. He wants to see proof this will actually get used and drive results.

questions_asked.question: What would make Dakota confident this is different?
questions_asked.answer: Show him the ROI. He's a numbers guy. If you can prove the forecast accuracy improvement pays for itself, he'll buy in.

next_steps: Prepare ROI deck for Dakota
Levi to review technical requirements

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation with Levi Okafor. Walked through integration with Salesforce, Outlook, and Zoom. Levi asked detailed questions about data security, user permissions, and rollout process. He's concerned about change management with the sales team but likes the automation angle. Confirmed they'd need 29 seats for full sales org coverage.

questions_asked.question: How does the platform handle PII and healthcare data compliance?
questions_asked.answer: We're SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliant. All data is encrypted at rest and in transit, and you control retention policies.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical rollout timeline for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2 weeks for technical setup, then we recommend a phased rollout over 4 weeks to ensure adoption.

questions_asked.question: What's your biggest concern about rolling this out to the team?
questions_asked.answer: They're already drowning in tools. If this feels like more work, they'll ignore it. Needs to be truly automatic.

objections: Our team is already tool-fatigued. This needs to be zero friction.
next_steps: Levi to test sandbox environment
Schedule second technical session to review API access

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Finn with sandbox access for Levi and updated timeline. Finn confirmed Levi is testing this week and Dakota wants to see a live demo before they move forward.
next_steps: Schedule demo for Dakota
Levi to complete sandbox testing by Friday

touches.ai_summary: Second technical evaluation with Levi. He tested the sandbox and was impressed with the automation and deal health scoring. Asked about custom reporting, API rate limits, and Salesforce field mapping. Confirmed the integration meets their security requirements. He's ready to recommend moving forward but wants to see pricing before bringing it to Dakota.

questions_asked.question: Can we build custom dashboards for Dakota's exec reporting?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have a dashboard builder and can also work with you to create custom templates during onboarding.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need more than 29 seats later?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime at the same per-seat rate, prorated for the remainder of your contract term.

questions_asked.question: What would make you hesitate to recommend this to Finn and Dakota?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, just price. If it's reasonable, this solves a real problem for us. Fireflies was cheap but useless.

next_steps: Send pricing proposal for 29 seats
Schedule exec demo with Dakota

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Finn. Presented $26,700 ACV for 29 seats with unlimited automation tier. Walked through ROI model showing $180K in prevented slippage and 15 hours/week saved on forecast calls. Finn liked the numbers but mentioned Dakota will likely negotiate on price since Fireflies is significantly cheaper. Agreed to position this as a forecasting platform, not call recording.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to Fireflies pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies is a transcription tool. We're a forecasting and deal intelligence platform. The ROI is in the accuracy and time savings, not just transcripts.

questions_asked.question: What's your gut feeling on Dakota's reaction to this price?
questions_asked.answer: He'll push back. He's very cost-conscious. But if we can prove the ROI in the demo, he'll come around.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on contract length for better pricing?
questions_asked.answer: We can explore annual vs multi-year, but this is already our mid-market tier. Let me see what I can do.

objections: Dakota is going to compare this to Fireflies pricing and push back hard.
next_steps: Schedule exec meeting with Dakota for next week
Send formal proposal doc

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Dakota Richter. Demo went well—Dakota was impressed with the deal risk scoring and forecast accuracy features. He pushed back on price, saying Fireflies is 1/3 the cost. Explained the ROI difference but he asked for a discount to make the business case easier internally. Meeting was rescheduled once due to Dakota's conflict. He's willing to move forward but wants Ravi to review the contract terms first.

questions_asked.question: What's the discount structure if we commit to a multi-year deal?
questions_asked.answer: For a 2-year commitment we can offer 15% off, which brings your annual cost to around $22,700.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through the ROI model again. How confident are you in these numbers?
questions_asked.answer: It's based on your current 25% forecast miss rate and average deal size. Even a 10-point improvement saves you six figures in prevented slippage and board credibility.

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation risk? I don't want another Fireflies situation.
questions_asked.answer: We have a dedicated onboarding team and Levi's already validated the technical fit. We guarantee accuracy within 30 days or you can cancel.

objections: The price is steep compared to Fireflies. I need you to sharpen your pencil.
next_steps: Send revised proposal with multi-year discount options
Ravi to review contract terms and security questionnaire

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Finn with revised proposal showing 2-year and 3-year discount scenarios. Finn responded saying Dakota is leaning toward the 2-year option but Ravi has questions about auto-renewal terms and data export policies.
next_steps: Legal to address Ravi's contract questions
Schedule pricing call to finalize terms

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Finn. Discussed 2-year commitment at $22,700/year. Finn confirmed Dakota is comfortable at that price point but wants to confirm the unlimited automation tier includes custom reporting. Also asked about early termination clauses. Agreed to remove auto-renewal and include a 60-day out clause after year one.

questions_asked.question: If we don't hit the forecast accuracy targets, can we exit the contract early?
questions_asked.answer: We can include a performance clause—if we don't improve accuracy by 10 points in 90 days, you can terminate without penalty.

questions_asked.question: What's the approval process look like from here?
questions_asked.answer: I'll take the updated terms to Dakota this week. If he signs off, Ravi will handle the procurement paperwork.

next_steps: Send revised contract with performance clause and no auto-renewal
Finn to brief Dakota on updated terms

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Dakota to finalize terms. He approved the 2-year deal at $22,700/year with the performance guarantee. Asked about onboarding timeline and what happens if they want to expand to their customer success team later. Confirmed Ravi will need 1 week for procurement review and MSA negotiation.

questions_asked.question: What's the pricing if we add our 8-person CS team later?
questions_asked.answer: Same per-seat rate, prorated. For 8 seats that'd be about $7K additional annually.

questions_asked.question: When can we go live if we sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: Technical setup takes 2 weeks, then we'd phase in over 4 weeks. You'd have full rollout by mid-next month.

next_steps: Ravi to begin MSA review
Legal teams to align on data processing terms
